# RustyNES v2.1.1 — "Fathom" (patch)

A focused bug-fix patch on top of v2.1.0 "Fathom". The cycle-accurate core is unchanged — **AccuracyCoin stays 141/141 (100.00%)**, nestest 0-diff — and no accuracy/oracle number moves.

## Fixed — Wizards & Warriors (and ~1900 other games) froze at level load

Wizards & Warriors — and potentially any game on a mapper that switches its own nametable mirroring — froze a few seconds into a level on the desktop app **and** the WASM build: the playfield rendered only its top half, the status bar was blank, sprites (the character, the tree door) dropped or blinked, and audio + input stalled. A headless core capture always looked correct.

### Root cause: a bad row in the per-game database

RustyNES ships a per-game database (`game_database.txt`, vendored from TetaNES) that corrects known-bad iNES headers at load time. It force-applied its **mirroring** column to *every* recognized ROM — including mappers that control their own nametable mirroring in hardware.

**Wizards & Warriors is AxROM (mapper 7)**, which flips single-screen A↔B **mid-frame** (gated by a sprite-0 hit) to paint its bottom status bar from the second nametable. The database lists the game as `Horizontal` (a meaningless header bit for an AxROM cart). Force-applying `Horizontal` pinned the mirroring, so the status-bar half of the split rendered blank, the sprite-0 hit that drives the split stopped firing, and the game wedged in its sprite-0 wait loop. The deterministic core never consults the database, which is why a headless replay was always correct — the fault was injected by the frontend's load path.

### The fix

A game-database mirroring correction is now honored **only** for boards whose mirroring is genuinely hardwired by the cartridge (NROM / UxROM / CNROM / GxROM), via a new `Mapper::has_hardwired_mirroring()` capability. It **defaults to "the mapper controls its own mirroring"**, the safe direction — so no mapper-controlled board can ever be corrupted by a stray database value. This protects **1914** of the database's 2682 rows (every MMC1, MMC3, AxROM, MMC5, VRC, … entry) from the same class of freeze. See **ADR 0031**.

## Also fixed — a run-ahead PPU save-state gap

Run-ahead's per-frame `snapshot` + `restore` round-trip did not serialize some PPU render state — the per-sprite shifter-halt state (`spr_halted`), the 1-dot-delayed rendering gate (`prev_rendering_enabled` / `rendering_enabled_delayed`), and the OAM-row-corruption arming state — so a snapshot/restore could drift them. **`PPU_SNAPSHOT_VERSION` 5 → 6** adds an **additive** tail serializing those fields; pre-v6 `.rns` save-states still load (upconverting to power-on defaults — not an ADR-0028 epoch break), and the fix also hardens netplay rollback and manual save/load.

> This run-ahead change was originally believed to be the Wizards & Warriors freeze cause. Full core-state diffing later proved run-ahead was byte-identical and the freeze was the game-database mirroring override above; the snapshot fix remains a valid save-state-completeness improvement on its own.

## Notes

- Version: workspace `2.1.0 → 2.1.1`. No mobile / dependency changes.
- Regression tests: `hardwired_mirroring_gate_matches_board_type` (mirroring gate) and the GitHub-safe `ww_runahead_matches_plain_across_a_mid_frame_split` (skips cleanly when the commercial dump is absent).
- Full host gate green: fmt · clippy · rustdoc · no_std cross-compile · markdownlint · PPU + core snapshot/rollback suites.

Full detail: `CHANGELOG.md` `[2.1.1]`.
